gaps.before: Scarcely had the speaker finished her talk
gaps.after: the audience began to applaud loudly.
gaps.explanation: Scarcely had ... when ... で「〜するやいなや」。文頭の否定語句により倒置が起き、相関するのは when。

gaps.before: The new findings cast serious doubt
gaps.explanation: cast doubt on 〜 で「〜に疑いを投げかける」。doubt と結びつく前置詞は on。

gaps.after: , the team lacked the funding to carry it out.
gaps.explanation: 野心的な計画→だが資金不足、という逆接。前後が対立するので however が適切。

readingQuestions.prompt: 文脈に最も合う選択肢を選んでください。

gaps.before: The teacher encouraged her students to
gaps.after: their mistakes rather than hide them.
gaps.explanation: learn from one's mistakes 「失敗から学ぶ」。前置詞は from が正しい。

gaps.after: the heavy rain, the outdoor festival continued as planned.
gaps.explanation: 後ろが名詞句 the heavy rain なので前置詞 Despite が正しい。Although は節を導くので不可。
make a decision 「決断する」が定型表現。do/take/give は decision とは結びつかない。

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Surprising Minds of Octopuses
paragraphs: Octopuses look nothing like us, yet scientists increasingly describe them as remarkably intelligent. With eight arms, three hearts, and no bones, these soft creatures seem almost alien. Still, in laboratory tanks they solve puzzles, open jars, and even appear to recognize individual keepers.

What makes their intelligence so unusual is how it is organized. Unlike a human brain, much of an octopus's neural power is spread throughout its arms, allowing each limb to explore and react on its own. An octopus can taste with its skin and squeeze its entire body through a gap no wider than its eye. Some have even been seen carrying coconut shells to use later as shelter, a behavior that hints at planning.

Yet studying these animals is difficult. Octopuses are solitary and short-lived, rarely surviving more than a couple of years. Because they leave no traditions for the next generation to inherit, each octopus must learn the world almost entirely on its own. For researchers, that lonely brilliance is both a puzzle and a quiet reminder that intelligence can take many forms.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Octopuses show a strange but impressive intelligence that differs greatly from our own.
Octopuses are closely related to humans and think in exactly the same way.
Octopuses are unable to solve any problems in laboratory settings.
Octopuses live in large social groups and carefully teach their young.

questions.before: Unlike humans, an octopus keeps much of its thinking power

questions.prompt: オクトパスが将来のために計画している可能性を示す行動はどれですか。

spans.text: carrying coconut shells to use later

questions.prompt: 第3段落が伝えている主な点はどれですか。
options: Because octopuses live alone and only briefly, each one must learn about the world on its own.
Octopuses pass detailed traditions down to their young over many generations.
Octopuses usually live for several decades in the wild.
Researchers find octopuses unusually easy to study.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: Testing the Four-Day Week
paragraphs: Several companies around the world have begun experimenting with a shorter working week. Instead of the usual five days, employees work four, often for the same pay. Supporters believe that well-rested workers can do just as much in less time.

Early trials have produced encouraging results. In many cases, productivity stayed the same or even rose, while workers reported less stress and more time for family and hobbies. Some businesses also noticed that fewer people quit, saving the cost of hiring and training new staff.

Still, the idea does not suit everyone. Hospitals, shops, and factories often need staff present every day, and squeezing five days of work into four can leave some employees feeling rushed. Whether the four-day week becomes common may depend on the type of job rather than on enthusiasm alone.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: The four-day week shows real promise but may not fit every kind of job.
The four-day week has already replaced the five-day week everywhere.
Shorter weeks always reduce how much work actually gets done.
Workers generally dislike having more free time.

questions.before: According to the passage, one benefit reported by workers was

questions.sentence: For these workplaces, someone must be on duty at all times.

listeningQuestions.transcript: I'd appreciate it if you could send the report by the end of the day.
The flight to Toronto leaves at a quarter past seven tomorrow morning.
Had I known about the delay, I would have taken an earlier bus.
We decided to plant a few tomatoes on the balcony this spring.
The gentle breeze carried the scent of fresh bread through the quiet village.
Do you happen to know whether the library stays open on weekends?
